name: BuscaChat - Bot de WA/T para reencuentro de famlias
db_description:
# BuscaChat

BuscaChat es un bot de Telegram y WhatsApp (En proceso) que ayuda a buscar y reportar personas desaparecidas tras la tragedia de los terremotos ocurridos en Venezuela.

# Objetivo del Proyecto
BuscaChat tiene como objetivo brindar una herramienta rápida, accesible y confiable que permita a las personas afectadas por los terremotos localizar a sus familiares y seres queridos, facilitando la identificación de personas desaparecidas, encontradas, heridas o trasladadas a refugios y hospitales, contribuyendo así a la reunificación familiar durante una emergencia humanitaria.

# ¿Como Funciona?
BuscaChat funciona como un bot de Telegram y WhatsApp (En proceso) que permite buscar y registrar personas afectadas por una emergencia de forma rápida y sencilla. Cuando un usuario realiza una búsqueda, el sistema consulta simultáneamente múltiples fuentes de información, unifica y elimina registros duplicados para mostrar los resultados más relevantes. Además, cualquier persona puede reportar nuevos casos de personas desaparecidas o encontradas, los cuales se incorporan a la base de datos y quedan disponibles para futuras consultas, facilitando la colaboración comunitaria y la reunificación familiar.

# Equipo
- Diego Mucha Alvarez 
- Andrea Granados García
- Rebeca Castro Pineda
- Juan Guevara Bustamante
- Giovanny Valbuena
- Luis Fernandez
- Neptali Piña
- Sergio Loza
Mas información sobre el equipo: https://acortar.link/ypHfAV

# Arquitectura
  Usuario → Telegram → Zavu → Railway (/webhook)
  → FastAPI → router → handler → Zavu API → Telegram

  Lenguaje:  Python 3.14
  Framework: FastAPI + uvicorn
  Plataforma: Zavu (multicanal)
  HTTP:      aiohttp
  DB:        SQLite (MVP)
  Hosting:   Railway (Docker)
  Tests:     pytest (103 tests)

# Enlaces
- Bot Telegram:   https://t.me/BuscaChatVzla_bot
- Bot WhatsApp: https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=584220300918&text=Hola,+me+gustaría%20buscar%20una%20persona%20por%20nombre%20o%20cédula
- Código Repo 1: https://github.com/DiegoMucha/BuscaChat
- Código Repo 2: https://github.com/gfurion/Buscachat-Telegram/

architecture.pattern: Layered service architecture (FastAPI routers -> messaging pipeline/services -> SQLModel data layer), with adapter pattern for external integrations (WhatsApp Meta Cloud API, Venezuela Te Busca API, face matching engines)
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: SQLModel (SQLAlchemy 2.x) with Alembic migrations, raw psycopg3 driver, pgvector extension for face embedding similarity search
architecture.api_design: FastAPI REST + webhook endpoints (WhatsApp Meta webhook with HMAC signature verification, web-chat tester endpoint, health checks); no public browsable REST API beyond that
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Strong: routers/ (HTTP), services/ (business logic: search, ocr, bot_intake), messaging/ (conversation state machine, pipeline, adapters per channel), adapters/ (external API clients), face/ (pluggable matcher interface with stub and insightface implementations), models.py (schema)
architecture.modularity: Good modularity via dependency injection (FastAPI Depends), pluggable interfaces for face matcher, notifier, and conversation state store (in-memory vs Redis) selected by config
notable_patterns: Adapter pattern for messaging channels (Meta WhatsApp, web chat) unified via app/messaging/types.py
Strategy pattern for face matcher (stub vs insightface) and notifier (null vs green_api)
Idempotency/dedupe via unique message_id table for webhook messages
OpenTelemetry instrumentation wired for FastAPI/httpx/psycopg/redis/sqlalchemy with Honeycomb export
Background task processing for webhook messages (FastAPI BackgroundTasks) to ack quickly and process async

code_organization.directory_structure: Clean, conventional FastAPI project layout: app/{routers,services,messaging,adapters,face,utils}, alembic/ for migrations, tests/ mirroring source, deploy/ and scripts/ for ops, plus a separate landing-page/ static site
code_organization.naming_quality: Consistent, descriptive snake_case naming; clear module boundaries (bot_intake.py, session_store.py, web_chat_store.py, insightface_matcher.py)
code_organization.documentation_quality: Bilingual (Spanish) README with setup, endpoints, telemetry doc (TELEMETRY.md), AGENTS.md for AI-agent contribution rules, and a project vision doc (Bot_salva_vidas.md)
code_organization.reasoning: Directory layout, naming, and docs reflect a deliberately engineered codebase rather than hackathon scaffolding; migrations are incremental and named meaningfully (e.g. 006_drop_sync_tables, 007_drop_webhook_event_logs) showing real iteration.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: Secrets via .env (gitignored) and GitHub Actions secrets (SERVER_SSH_DEPLOY_KEY); Meta webhook validates HMAC-SHA256 signature (x-hub-signature-256) against app secret; verify token for webhook subscription handshake; PRIVATE_API_TOKEN placeholder present. No JWT/user auth needed since it's a bot service, but inbound webhook auth is properly implemented.
production_readiness.security_notes: Meta webhook signature verification uses hmac.compare_digest (timing-safe); chat identifiers are hashed (SHA-256/HMAC) before logging to avoid leaking raw phone numbers in logs; no obvious SQL injection risk since SQLModel/SQLAlchemy query builder is used throughout (no raw string interpolation in queries besides parameterized func.replace). Deploy relies on SSH key + Cloudflare Access tunnel rather than exposing SSH publicly.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Single-service deploy via systemd unit + docker-compose Postgres/Redis, not containerized/orchestrated (no Kubernetes/multi-instance setup); background tasks run in-process via FastAPI BackgroundTasks (not a durable queue), so long-running jobs could be lost on crash; conversation state can be swapped to Redis for horizontal scale but defaults to in-memory single-instance store.
production_readiness.reasoning: Real deploy pipeline (GitHub Actions -> SSH via Cloudflare Access -> systemd service with rolling restart script), health checks, OpenTelemetry/Honeycomb observability, webhook signature verification, and structured logging with PII hashing are all present and functioning — well beyond hackathon-typical scaffolding, though it is a single-VM deployment without redundancy or a durable job queue.

maturity.reasoning: 73 commits from at least a few active contributors, 1174+ lines of tests covering conversation flow, face matching, OCR, search, webhook signature verification, and an external adapter with mocked fixtures (including real e2e tests using testcontainers); CI/CD workflow performs an actual remote deploy over SSH with retries; TELEMETRY.md and AGENTS.md show ongoing engineering discipline rather than one-shot AI scaffolding.

viability.reasoning: The project already integrates with a real external missing-persons dataset (Venezuela Te Busca / SOS Venezuela sync), supports WhatsApp as the primary channel Venezuelans actually use, has working face-matching (pgvector + InsightFace) to help reunite families from photos, and is already deployed to a production server with monitoring — this is close to field-ready for earthquake victim search, needing mainly WhatsApp Business API approval/scale testing and durable job queueing for reliability at scale.

key_features: WhatsApp chatbot (Meta Cloud API) for searching missing persons by name or cedula
Facial recognition matching (pgvector + InsightFace embeddings, 512-dim, HNSW index) to compare submitted photos against reported missing people
Integration/sync adapter with external 'Venezuela Te Busca' and SOS Venezuela missing-persons databases
OCR service for scanning Venezuelan cedula (ID) documents
Conversational state machine with pluggable in-memory or Redis session store for multi-turn bot conversations
Web-based chat tester UI (separate from WhatsApp) for local testing
Webhook deduplication/idempotency and background async processing of inbound messages
OpenTelemetry + Honeycomb observability and structured logging with hashed PII
Automated CI/CD deployment pipeline to a production VM via GitHub Actions + Cloudflare Access SSH tunnel
Static landing page for the project
analysis.merge_potential: High-value standalone service that other hackathon projects (e.g. shelter-mapping, volunteer-coordination) could integrate with via its missing-persons DB and WhatsApp bot, or by consuming its person-search API/adapter; its face-matching and OCR pipeline could be reused as a shared microservice.
red_flags: Background job processing relies on in-process FastAPI BackgroundTasks rather than a durable queue, risking silent loss of in-flight messages on deploy/crash
Single-VM deployment (systemd + docker-compose) with no visible redundancy/failover for a life-critical service
Face matcher defaults to a lightweight 'stub' in dev/tests; real InsightFace matching is an optional dependency group that must be explicitly installed and enabled in production
Meta webhook signature verification silently passes (returns True) when app_secret is not configured, which could allow unauthenticated webhook calls if misconfigured in production
analysis.summary: Buscachat is a genuinely production-grade FastAPI service that powers a WhatsApp chatbot to help Venezuelans search for and report missing earthquake victims, combining a real external missing-persons dataset sync, pgvector-based facial recognition matching, cedula OCR, and a conversational pipeline, all backed by Postgres/Redis, Alembic migrations, OpenTelemetry observability, an extensive test suite, and an actual CI/CD deploy pipeline to a live server.
analysis.project_type: Backend service (FastAPI chatbot/API) with an accompanying static landing page

frontend: Server-rendered minimal web-chat tester via FastAPI

diffusion.recommended_angle: A real WhatsApp/Telegram bot that uses facial recognition and a live missing-persons database to reunite Venezuelan earthquake victims with their families — frame it as 'search for your loved one from your phone in seconds.'
gaps: No demo video showing the bot actually finding/matching a person end-to-end
Landing page itself has no embedded interactive demo — CTAs just link out to WhatsApp/Telegram
No screenshots of real bot conversations or face-match results
No visible evidence yet of real users/successful reunifications to cite as proof

evaluation.live_demo_notes: The live_url (https://ayudandoavenezuela.netlify.app/#) is a static marketing landing page (three-step explainer: 'Inicia el chat', 'Sube la foto', 'Recibe respuesta', plus privacy/mission copy) — it does NOT embed a working search or chat interface. It links out to a Telegram bot (https://t.me/BuscaChatVzla_bot) and a WhatsApp number, which is where the actual product lives; those were not independently verified via WebFetch in this pass. So the promotable public asset (the landing page) is not itself the working demo — the working demo is the bot, one click away.
evaluation.one_line_pitch: BuscaChat: manda una foto por WhatsApp o Telegram y su IA de reconocimiento facial te ayuda a reencontrar a tu familia tras el terremoto.
evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: Family reunification after mass-casualty disasters (earthquakes) is one of the most acute and time-critical needs for victims — every hour of delay in identifying missing/found/injured people compounds trauma and risk. BuscaChat directly targets this via a WhatsApp/Telegram bot (channels Venezuelans already use, unlike a web app requiring separate adoption), integrates with a real external missing-persons dataset ('Venezuela Te Busca'/SOS Venezuela), and adds facial recognition (pgvector + InsightFace) and cedula OCR to make search/report low-friction for panicked, possibly low-bandwidth users. This is squarely a life-critical problem, not a nice-to-have.
